description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who needs to prepare a quick summary of how many servers of each role are present in an internal inventory.  
The inventory file already exists on the machine:

    /home/user/provisioning/inventory.csv

Its exact, complete contents are:

    web-01,web,prod
    web-02,web,prod
    db-01,db,prod
    cache-01,cache,prod
    db-02,db,staging
    web-03,web,staging
    cache-02,cache,staging
    queue-01,queue,prod
    queue-02,queue,staging
    queue-03,queue,dev
    web-04,web,dev
    db-03,db,dev

Column layout of inventory.csv  
1 = hostname  2 = role  3 = environment  

Task  
1. Using classic Unix text-processing utilities (for example: cut, sort, uniq, etc.) create a frequency report that counts how many times each unique ROLE (column 2) appears in the file.  
2. Write the result to a brand-new file located at

    /home/user/provisioning/report/role_frequency.log

   If necessary, create the /home/user/provisioning/report directory first.  
3. The output file must contain one line per unique role, strictly in the format:

    <role>,<count>

   • Lines must be sorted by descending count (largest first).  
   • If two roles have the same count, they must then be ordered alphabetically (byte-wise) by the role name.  
   • Do not include any header line; only the data lines.  
   • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.  

Important verification rules used by the automated grader  
• The file must exist exactly at /home/user/provisioning/report/role_frequency.log.  
• It must be world-readable (chmod 644 or stricter for group/others read-only is fine).  
• Content must exactly match the specified ordering and formatting; no extra whitespace, no extra lines.  
• Only the count of column 2 (role) is required; do not include environments or hostnames.

While performing the task, feel free to display intermediate console output to check your work, but remember only the final file will be graded.
truth:
Directory and file that must exist before the agent starts:

/home/user/provisioning/inventory.csv  – contains exactly the 12 lines shown in the public task description.

Expected artefacts that must be created by the agent:

1. Directory:
/home/user/provisioning/report
   • permissions: 755 or 750 acceptable.

2. File:
/home/user/provisioning/report/role_frequency.log
   • permissions: 644 or 640 acceptable.
   • exact byte content (with a single trailing newline):

web,4
db,3
queue,3
cache,2

   • There should be no BOM, no carriage returns.

Automated tests will:

1. Verify that role_frequency.log exists and is readable.
2. Read its contents and compare byte-for-byte to the expected four-line block above (including the trailing newline).
name: task_000000_112d2dd2
